content.full_text:
Eckel for Council

P.O. Box 1601                          Campaign Managers:
Danville, CA 94526-6601               Finance and Issues – Ken Cox
Home: (415) 838-9387                  Operations – Betty Costello
Office: (415) 947-4709                Administration – Sabrina Tallon

Campaign Consultant – Tom Jones


October 24, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Today in completing my California 1991 Form 490 filing for the period 9/22-10/19/91 covering my candidacy for Councilmember in the Town of Danville, I discussed an expenditure for an annual individual membership and processing fee of $189.00 which I paid from my campaign funds. The purpose of this expenditure was to join the Danville Area Chamber of Commerce.

After researching this matter, one of your associates, Mr. John Wallace, called me back and confirmed that, under current FPPC regulations, he interpreted this expenditure as a legitimate one, and recommended that I confer with the Technical division for appropriate directions on filing.

Subsequent to my conversation with Mr. Wallace, I conferred by telephone today again with Alice Hughes who agreed that this expenditure should be declared on Schedule E and described in the "Description of Payment" column as I had already done. I have since filed the original and one copy of this period's Form 490 with the Town of Danville.

In order to prevent any problems in the future, I would be indebted to you if you would have Mr. Wallace send me a written confirmation of his advice of today.

Sincerely,

Christopher A. Eckel
Christopher A. Eckel

Paid for by the Eckel for Council Committee, ID# 9109333,
P.O. Box 1601, Danville, CA 94526-6601, Sabrina Tallon, Treasurer

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank document image with very faint or minimal text content. The image is predominantly white/light gray with only a few barely visible marks or characters scattered across it, including what appears to be some text in the upper left area and upper right area, but the content is too faint or unclear for me to reliably transcribe accurately.

If you could provide a clearer or higher contrast version of this document, I would be able to provide a more accurate transcription.
